title: Pilot cancer-site explainer (provenanced, 'not medical advice,' verified helplines) — oncologist + advocate signed off
context: This is the flagship patient-facing explainer for the pilot cancer site selected in incidence-explorers-site-002. Any content that a patient or family member reads about a specific cancer is HIGH risk-tier and requires sign-off from a credentialed oncologist AND a patient advocate before it ships — no sign-off, no ship. The explainer must carry persistent 'general education, not medical advice — talk to your care team' framing and use verified, dated crisis/helpline references from incidence-explorers-helplines-014. No partner, oncologist, or advocate is yet secured.
objective: Write the pilot cancer-site explainer: every quantitative claim cites its DataPoint (provenance complete); persistent 'general education, not medical advice — talk to your care team' framing throughout; no diagnosis/prognosis/individual-risk/screening/treatment guidance; content-safety lint passes; crisis/helpline references use the verified, dated entries from incidence-explorers-helplines-014 and are region-appropriate. The explainer must be signed off by a credentialed oncologist AND a patient advocate (version-scoped, recorded in the reviewers ledger) before it ships — blocking.
acceptanceCriteria: Every quantitative claim in the explainer cites its DataPoint with a complete, resolvable provenance record (source, release, query params, extraction date, URL/DOI, license, age-standard, measure); no uncited claim is present

Persistent 'general education, not medical advice — talk to your care team' framing is present on every patient-facing surface; no diagnosis, prognosis, individual-risk, screening, or treatment guidance is included
Crisis/helpline references use the verified, dated entries from incidence-explorers-helplines-014 and are region-appropriate and current

Signed off by a credentialed oncologist AND a patient advocate (version-scoped, with credentials and consent recorded in the reviewers ledger) before the explainer ships — this is a hard blocking gate
Plain-language readability target (~grade 8) met and a plain-language review passed
output: A published pilot cancer-site explainer — fully provenanced, 'not medical advice'-framed, with verified helplines, passing the content-safety lint and plain-language review, and signed off by a credentialed oncologist AND a patient advocate (version-scoped, recorded) — the first patient-facing deed of the project.
